name: Project Nebius
info: Nebius Group is redeveloping the former Regions Bank operations campus on Lakeshore Parkway into a 300 MW AI computing facility spanning roughly ~75–80 acres. Filed under permit BHM01 in January 2026 and marketed as the "Birmingham AI Factory," the project will demolish existing structures for a purpose-built GPU data center. It faces community opposition over power demand, noise, and proximity to residential neighborhoods, and is proceeding under pre-moratorium development rights while a city-wide pause on new large data centers is in effect.

name: Project Marvel
address: Off Rock Mountain Lake Road, near the I-459/I-20/59 interchange
info: Logistics Land Investment LLC is proposing an 18-building, 4.5 million sq ft hyperscale complex targeting 1.2 GW of power capacity. Originally planned on ~700 acres rezoned from agricultural to light industrial in November 2025, the project expanded in early 2026 to a revised master plan totaling approximately 1,600 acres. The $14.5 billion project has drawn sustained community opposition over environmental impact, energy use, and transparency concerns, and multiple rezoning and planning steps remain before construction can proceed.

name: Meta Platforms Montgomery Data Center
address: Off I‑65 near the Hyundai plant
companies: Terracon; Hensel Phelps Construction; Meta; Aldridge Electric
info: Meta is constructing a massive 715,000 sq ft AI data center off I-65, near the Hyundai assembly plant, representing a $800 million investment. Initially announced in May 2024 as an $800 million, 715,000 sq ft facility, the project was subsequently expanded, bringing the total investment to $1.5 billion and the campus to over 1.3 million sq ft. Powered entirely by renewable energy and targeting LEED Gold certification, the facility is expected to come online by the end of 2026 and will support approximately 100 permanent jobs. Meta purchased several lots totalling roughly 1,453 acres for $34.88 million. A $300 million, 4,500-acre solar farm is being developed by Silicon Ranch in Stockton, Alabama, to power this data center.

name: DC BLOX Montgomery
info: DC BLOX has plans for a 80,000 square foot, 5 MW data center in Montgomery's Interstate Industrial Park, and plans to expand to 40 MW in the future. The announcement came in November 2024, and no news has broke since. A non-profit, non-goverrnmental body, Montgomery Area Chamber of Commerce, has secured what has so far been DC Blox's committment to invest $30 million in the project. We were unable to find any discussion on the matter in any Montogmery City Council meeting minutes, and the land is currently owned by the Water works and Sanity Sewer Board of Montgomery.

name: DC BLOX Birmingham
companies: DC BLOX; Alabama Power; Jefferson County Economic and Industrial Development Authority
info: DC BLOX is exapnding their Birmingham data center to roughly 263,000 square feet. Plans appear to include a substation, but we have not confirmed any details regarding its construction at this time. In 2019, The Birmingham City Council rezoned the land from Mixed Use Medium to General Commercial for the first phase of the data center. The location is built on what was previously the Trinity Park steel plant, which is a brownfield site.

name: AUBix + Core Scientific Data Center
info: Core Scientific is investing $400 million to expand its Auburn data center, which will be hosted within the existing AUBix site. At launch the data center will have a capacity of 16 MW.

name: Childersburg Data Center
info: Two undisclosed groups have expressed interest in developing a data center somewhere the Childersburg Industrial Park. As of April 2026, no property has been sold, no plans have been approved.

name: Tallassee Mill Data Center
address: Former Tallasee Mill site, along the Tallapoosa River near Thurlow Dam
info: Two developers have expressed interest in redeveloping the former Tallassee Mill into a data center. The proposal has been described as 10 MW, but with potential expansion to 20 MW. This proposal has drawn fierce scrutiny, as the Tallassee Mill is a historic mill from 1853. Tallassee's mayor is referenced mentioning this project could bring 15-30 well-paying jobs. Land and zoning status are unknown.

name: Project Red Clay
info: Cloverleaf Infrastructure is planning data center development, nicknamed "Project Red Clay," on a roughly 800-acre site near Highway 80 and Highway 21 in Burkville. \n According to the developer, this project represents an over $1 billion  taxable investment in Lowndes County. During the 3+ year construction period, more than 1,000 jobs will be created, and once operational, the facility will support at least 50 permanent full-time positions. The project will use a closed-loop cooling system designed by licensed Alabama engineers that minimizes daily water consumption, and any necessary power and transmission upgrades will be fully funded by the project so existing ratepayers see no increase in their electricity bills.

name: Edged Energy Prichard Data Center
info: Edged Energy is proposing a data center somewhere on Telegraph Road. This project represents an estimated $93 million investment into the city, and is expected to create approximately 20 high-paying jobs, with salaries exceeding $70,000 per year.

name: Columbiana Mining Facility and Data Center
companies: Digi Power X; US Data Centers
info: Digi Power X (formerly known as Digihost) is developing an AI and high-performance computing (HPC) data center at 130 Industrial Parkway in Columbiana. The company purchased the approximately 160,000-square-foot industrial property in June 2022 for $2.75 million and initially used it for bitcoin mining. The project is planned in two phases with a combined capacity of 55 MW: Phase 1 will deliver 22 MW and is targeted for completion in late 2026, while Phase 2 will add 33 MW by early 2027

name: Project Leo
info: AVAIO Digital Group has announced a $6 billion data center hub outside Little Rock, supposedly off 145th Street. AVAIO is currently contracted with Entergy Arkansas for 150 MW of power, but they anticipate power demand of up to 1 GW as the facility grows. According to the announcement, construction of the first phase of the project is expected to start in the first quarter of 2026 and will be complete and energized in June 2027.

name: Project Pyramid
info: Google is proposing a five building hyperscale data center campus on a 1,178-acre property representing a $4-10-billion investment. The data center campus will reportedly only take up around ~580 acres of the properties. The project received approval for a 30-year payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (PILOT) agreement. Simultaneously, Google plans to construct a 600 MW solar farm to power this proposal, and the Arkansas Public Service Commission’s approved a 350 MW energy storage system.

name: Clarksville Data Center
address: South of the Johnson Regional Medical Center
info: Serverfarm is building a $8 billion hyperscale data center on 135 acres near the Johnson Regional Medical Center. This campus would be composed of six 360,000-square-foot buildings, each at ~64 MW.

name: Port of Little Rock Data Center
address: Located in unnamed tributaries and adjacent wetlands to Fourche Bayou
info: Google and Willowbend Capital, LLC are proposing a data center campus comprised of five 285,300-square-foot buildings, totaling ~1.43 million square feet in total. The project is expected to draw more than 100 MW of power. The proposal also includes two office/support buildings and an electrical substation/switching station covering around 20 acres.

name: Vilonia Cryptomine
companies: Interstate Holdings Blockchain; Steve Landers Jr.
info: A proposal from Interstate Holdings Blockchain for a cryptomine was rejected and withdrawn near the Entergy substation in Vilonia.

name: Millstone Nuclear Power Station Data Center
address: On site of Millstone Nuclear Power Station
companies: NE Edge Data Centers; Dominion Energy
info: NE Edge Data Centers is proposing a data center complex on site of the Millstone Nuclear Power Station, allowing the developer to purchase and draw electricity directly from the plant, which would occupy less than 5% of Millstone’s 526-acre site. The proposal consists of an electric switchyard and two 2-story data center facilities spanning an area of roughly 1.5 million square feet.

name: Wallingford Data Center
address: Watershed Interchange District at 1181 Barnes Road
info: Charter Development, as Charter Development Group LLC, submitted an application to modify Wallingford's zoning for watershed interchange (WI) district zones to include data centers as a permitted use in 2024.

name: Bloomfield Data Center Proposal
companies: Atlas Capital Group / Gotspace Data LLC
info: Atlas Capital Group is proposing a data center in Bloomfield that would feature a The project would include over 900,000 square feet of data processing space and a 127,000-square-foot substation. This project is anticipated to create ~40 jobs.

name: Trumbull Data Center Investment
info: Not a new construction project, but a large reinvestment ($200M) into an existing data center facility to attract more capacity and AWS/AI tenants; supported by municipality via tax incentive program.

name: Bristol Data Center
address: 234 Riverside Avenue, on the former Dodge Village site
info: GWDC Bristol LLC is proposing a 2-story data center and fuel cell at the former Dodge Village site, where the building sat vacant for about 15 years before being demolished in 2024. A developer from ReNew Developers LLC announced the project in 2023, planning a roughly $50 million investment in a 40,000 square foot facility powered by a fuel cell system with up to 4 MW of capacity. The fuel cell was approved by Connecticut's Siting Council in December 2022, with a construction extension granted in September 2025, and the data center will draw power through a dedicated Eversource gas feed. Bristol Mayor Ellen Zoppo-Sassu has framed the project as a brownfield redevelopment opportunity, noting the site drew no interest at auction and the water usage will be self-contained and recycled within the systems.

name: Project Washington
info: In March 2026, a state board unanimously upheld a decision from the Environmental Secretary that this project violates the Coastal Zone Act as it is located too close to shorelines. It is delayed while the developer decides to escalate to higher courts or withdraw it. Starwood Digital Ventures has proposed a 1.2 GW, 6-million-square-foot hyperscale data center campus off River Road. This site, which requires zoning change prior to approval, would be comprised of 11 2-story buildings. As of February 2026, the project has been delayed after the Delaware Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control (DNREC) deemed it not permitted under the Coastal Zone Act due to excessive diesal generator risks. The developer has 14 days to appeal this decision.

name: St. Georges Business Park Data Center Development  / "Frightland Project"
address: Near Port Penn Road; Frightland (Halloween-themed attraction)
info: Parkway Gravel Inc. is proposing the St. Georges Business Park in Middletown, just south of Port Penn Road. The complete project would redevelop nearly ~1500 acres and may require demolition of the Frightland Halloween attraction. Current exploratory plans call for approximately 3.24 million square feet of warehouse space across three large buildings. The southern portion of the property is planned as the Lighthouse Farms residential development, consisting of a mix of townhomes and single-family homes. The commercial buildings are planned as large warehouse facilities; data center use is not specified in the planning documents. The mapped area referenced represents only the northern portion of the overall parcel, where the warehouse buildings are proposed.

name: White Clay Creek Business Park Data Center Redevelopment
address: 100 White Clay Creek Business Park, off Ogletown Road
info: Developers are proposing a redevelopment that would demolish seven existing buildings on a 44-acre site called White Clay Center Industrial Park to replace them with three light industrial buildings and a guard shack for use as a data center campus. Because the property lies outside Newark’s municipal boundaries, it will be reviewed through New Castle County’s development process.

name: Project Zodiac
info: Google was granted a 10 year, 50% tax abatement from the Fort Wayne City Council following rezoning approval for 650 acres of land in Allen County by the Allen County Board of Commissioners in December 2023. The hyperscale data center project called Project Zodiac encompasses a total of 850 acres of land in total. A recent permit request was made acoording to local news to add 140 diesel generators on the site as backup, although there is no public record of an offical capacity for the site.

name: Bristol Data Center
address: South of Rail Park Drive, On County Rd 15 and 23
info: Preliminary activity south of Bristol Indiana has been underway since September 2024 when initial attempts to rezone Wilhelm Farm to M-1 was not passed. However since August 7th, 2025 when the Bristol Town Hall approved rezoning of roughly 170 acres from A-1 to M-1. On March 5th, 2026, the town hall also held a meeting where they discussed annexing 500 acres of agricultural land into Bristol. Residents fear that this land, which lies adjacent to the Wilhelm Farm, will likewise be used for future data centers. No developer has been named at this time.

name: Granger Data Center
address: Off Capital Avenue and Cleveland Rd
info: Microsoft purchased more than 900 acres of land on January 1st, 2025, months before St. Joseph City Hall approved the rezoning of a small section of the land for industrial development. In the same meeting, it also approved for the land to be developed specifically for data centers. Estimates for energy usage currently top 1 GW. Microsoft purchased the land for about $1.8 million.

name: South Bend Data Center
address: Prarie Ave and US-20, South West Corner of South Bend
info: A member of the South Bend Common Committee, Oliver Davis, was presented documents of plans to build a data center on a 300 acre plot in the south west corner of the city of South Bend. The plot of land, owned by Ceres Farms LLC., is already zoned industrial. Local news reports that the developers responsible for the rejected proposal in New Carlisle are the same involved in the South Bend plans, Western Hospitality Partners.

name: Decennial Group Akron Data Center
info: Decennial Group, a real estate investment firm, is proposing a 500 MW data center on 300 acres of land in Akron. The currently no site has been selected or announced publicly.

name: Project Rainier
info: AWS is partnering with Anthropic to supply upwards of half a million Trainium2 chips for training their AI models. The public announcement came in December 2024 during AWS' cloud computing expo, re:Invent. Prior to the official announcement, the St. Joseph County Redevelopment Commission began discussing preliminary activity in a November 9th, 2023. In their original meeting, they passed a resolution permitting Razor5 LLC to perform land development on the lower section of the site, a 680 acre plot. In May, 2024 Razor5 LLC purchased both site locations, totalling 850 acres. Amazon later publicized its deal with NIPSCO, the northern Indiana utility company, to provide their data center campus with 2.4 GW of electricity. Amazon cites that it will ultimately invest upwards of $15 billion into the site once it is fully completed.

name: Martindale Indianapolis Metrobloks Data Center
info: Metrobloks is proposing a $500 million, 154,000-square-foot data center on a ~14 acre site in the Martindale-Brightwood neighborhood, at the former Sherman Drive-In site. This data center facility will offer 72 MW of IT capacity and will use a closed-loop cooling system. Metropolitan Development Commission approved the rezoning 6-2 in April 2026, despite strong community opposition. The recommendation heads to the City-County Council for final approval, though the district councilor has publicly supported the project.

name: Surge Development Knightstown Data Center
address: Near intersection of State Road 109 and Interstate 70
info: Surge Development LLC and GM Development LLC are developing a hyperscale data center on ~585 acres of land near the intersection of Interstate 70 and State Road 109. In January 2026, Henry County Commissioners voted 2-1 to approve the Planned Unit Development outline. No specific tenant has been publicized. This is the second development in Henry County that Surge has been approved for.

name: Decatur Technology Park
address: Intersection of Kentucky Avenue and Camby Road
info: Sabey Data Centers is proposing a 250 MW data center campus comprised of two 900,000-square-foot buildings on 130 acres of land near the intersection of Kentucky Avenue and Camby Road. This project represents an investment of up to $4 billion.Groundbreaking is expected for 2026, with phased opening through 2028-2030.

name: Project Louie
address: Bounded by Keller Hill Road, Antioch Road, State Road 42, and West Union Church Road
info: Google is proposing "Project Louie", a hyperscale data center campus just east of Monrovia in Monroe Township, bounded roughly by Keller Hill Road (north), Antioch Road (east), State Road 42 (south), and West Union Church Road (west). County commissioners originally rezoned approximately 391 acres for the project in February, later approving the rezoning of an additional 158 acres, and Google separately purchased a 79-acre farm within Monrovia's town limits, bringing its total land holdings to approximately 628 acres. The project drew significant public opposition over energy and water demands and the use of nondisclosure agreements among county officials, though local leadership was ultimately more receptive than those in Franklin Township (withdrawn Project Flo), approving the rezoning and development agreement.

name: Hancock County MegaSite
info: Surge Development withdrew their proposal for a 775-acre data center campus, referred to as the Hancock County MegaSite, near Tuttle Orchards.

name: Project Flo
address: North CR 400 West at West CR 500 North
info: Google withdrew its proposal "Project Flo", a $1 billion, 468-acre data center campus on the southeast side of Indianapolis. Google negotiated through a holding company called Deep Meadow Ventures LLC, keeping their identity hidden behind non-disclosure agreements while they sought to rezone agricultural and residential land along I-74 and Post Road into a commercial special district to house four data centers. The project was expected to take 5-10 years to complete, and was expected to create 350 full-time jobs after completion. The proposal faced fierce opposition, leading Google to abruptly withdraw the proposal, minutes before the Indianapolis City-County Council was set to vote. This is possibly one of the first successful citizen campaigns against a major AI data center project in the United States.

name: Pike Township Data Center
address: Near West 79th Street and Township Line Road
info: American Tower Corporation has withdrawn their plans for a 4,400-square-foot data center near 79th Street and Township Line Road.

name: Project Maize
info: Project Maize is an $832 million data center campus being developed on the 70-acre former Federal-Mogul (ANCO) windshield-wiper plant at 402 Royal Road in Michigan City, Indiana. Originally initiated by Milwaukee-based Phoenix Investors, which has owned the site since 2022, the project involves redeveloping the long-vacant industrial brownfield into a grid-connected data center campus, with roof-raise and utility upgrades currently underway. Google formally acquired the project in April 2026, transitioning it from a third-party industrial redevelopment into a hyperscale campus for the tech giant, with Google taking responsibility for 100% of the site's electricity costs and any infrastructure requirements stemming from its operations. \n The Michigan City Common Council approved four resolutions advancing the project in September 2025. Local approvals include a 10-year, 50% real property tax abatement, with the project expected to create at least 30 permanent technical positions and up to 800 peak construction jobs.

name: Hobart Tech Park
info: Amazon is developing a large data center complex in Hobart, Indiana, as part of a broader $15 billion investment in northern Indiana for AI infrastructure. The facility is planned for a 700-plus-acre site at Colorado Avenue and 61st Avenue, with construction expected to begin in 2025 and a completion date of 2028. The city council unanimously approved the project, granting Amazon a 10-year tax abatement and a 35-year tax exemption on IT equipment, while Amazon provided a $47 million community enhancement payment and agreed to cover all infrastructure costs. \n The project has drawn significant local opposition, with residents raising concerns over health impacts, noise, water and electricity consumption, and proximity to a residential area. Transparency has also been a point of contention, as no environmental impact study or site renderings have been publicly released. Despite a lawsuit filed by some residents, Hobart's Plan Commission approved two site plans totaling more than 750 acres, and the project is expected to bring over 400 permanent jobs once complete.

name: Tract Project West
address: West of Highway 28 and south of Delaware Street
info: Project West is a proposed data center campus on roughly 282 acres in Norwalk's Southwest Development Corridor, west of Highway 28, south of Delaware Street, and north of the North River, in Warren County. The site is being developed by data center park developer Tract, which is buying the land from private owners and funding about $8.2 million in water and street infrastructure. The city anticipates a total project investment of around $12 billion, potentially the largest private investment in Warren County history, backed by an unnamed technology company. The Norwalk City Council unanimously approved the land and infrastructure development agreement on March 5, 2026, stressing that it is only a land/infrastructure agreement and that any actual data center would require an entirely separate approval process. Projected taxable valuation at full buildout is estimated at $400–500 million (generating about $15 million annually in new taxes, $5 million to the city), with an estimated 80 full-time positions and 600 construction jobs over a multi-year buildout. Developers requested no tax incentives.

name: Google Cedar Rapids
info: Google's Cedar Rapids data center is an approved hyperscale campus under construction along Edgewood Road SW in the Big Cedar Industrial Center. Developed through Heaviside LLC, which acquired 414 acres for about $16.6 million, it was approved by the Iowa Economic Development Authority in 2024 with roughly $56 million in tax incentives over 20 years and a commitment to at least 31 jobs. In May 2025 Google announced an additional $7 billion investment expanding its Iowa footprint; the first building is expected to be completed in 2026.

name: QTS Cedar Rapids
info: The QTS Cedar Rapids data center is an approved campus under construction on 612 acres within Alliant Energy's Big Cedar Industrial Center, immediately adjacent to Google's data center site. QTS Data Centers, a Blackstone portfolio company, was confirmed as the developer in a joint City of Cedar Rapids, Alliant Energy, and QTS announcement on February 13, 2025, following the City Council's approval of a development agreement with QTS Cedar Rapids LLC on January 28, 2025; under that agreement QTS will contribute up to $18 million over 20 years to the city's Community Betterment Fund. Initially announced as a minimum $750 million project, it was expanded in August 2025 into a commitment QTS has put at up to $10 billion, with plans for seven data center buildings and a water-free cooling system. Ground was broken on the first building in August 2025, with the first phase expected to be operational in 2026.

name: Meta Davenport Data Center
address: Northwest corner of Hillandale Road and Enterprise Way
info: Meta's Davenport data center is an approved two-building campus spanning 328 acres at the northwest corner of Hillandale Road and Enterprise Way. Developed through Meta subsidiary Vemerald LLC, it represents an $800 million investment with about 715,000 square feet of data halls and is expected to create 35 full-time jobs. The Iowa Economic Development Authority approved the project in May 2024, and the Davenport City Council approved a 20-year, 60% property tax exemption valued at about $161 million.

name: Project Ruthenium
info: Project Ruthenium is Microsoft's sixth West Des Moines data center, planned on about 103 acres at the southwest corner of Veterans Parkway and Southwest 60th Street. Microsoft acquired the site for roughly $15.4 million, and the West Des Moines City Council approved a development agreement in May 2024. Phase one calls for a 245,000-square-foot building and about $210 million in investment, with the site able to hold up to four buildings and creating about 25 full-time jobs. No tax abatement was provided. The project was first disclosed publicly to the City Council in November 2023.

name: Applied Digital Adair
address: Adjacent to NextEra substation, 1200 block of Fallow Avenue
info: Applied Digital has proposed a roughly 200 MW data center on land adjacent to a NextEra Energy substation in the 1200 block of Fallow Avenue. First reported in April 2024, the plan calls for two roughly 300,000-square-foot buildings on about 200 acres, representing an estimated $1.5 billion investment and 40 to 70 jobs, connected directly to the NextEra/MidAmerican supply. The proposal drew local opposition and discussion of a setback ordinance at county supervisor meetings in April 2024.

name: Edged Energy Des Moines
info: Edged Des Moines is a multi-tenant data center under construction on a 17.5-acre site at 9100 SE Four Mile Drive. Built by Edged, a subsidiary of Endeavour, the roughly 105,000-square-foot facility will deliver 13.2 MW using a closed-loop waterless cooling system, representing a $187 million investment and creating 22 jobs. The Ankeny City Council approved a development agreement including a five-year TIF package not to exceed $2 million. Edged announced the project in March 2025, broke ground in May 2025, topped out in August 2025, and expects to be operational by the end of 2026.

name: Project Morgan
info: Project Morgan is Apple's roughly 2000-acre data center campus at 2995 W Hickman Road. Announced in August 2017 by Apple CEO Tim Cook and Gov. Kim Reynolds as a $1.3-1.375 billion facility expected to create about 50 permanent jobs, the project was repeatedly delayed before its first building opened in November 2024. Apple's plan envisions up to six data center buildings totaling nearly 1.9 million square feet at full build-out. The company received more than $213 million in incentives, including roughly $194 million from Waukee and $20 million from the state.

name: Google Council Bluffs
info:
Google's Council Bluffs operation is one of Google's longest-running U.S. data centers and now spans two campuses. 
 The first facility, near Lake Manawa, began operations in 2009, and the second campus, known as Southlands, opened in 2013. The original campus is at 1430 Veterans Memorial Highway, while the larger Southlands campus is at 10420 Bunge Avenue. Plans were first announced in 2007, with further expansions announced in 2012, 2015, 2023, and 2024. 
 In July 2024 Google pledged another roughly $1 billion expansion for two new buildings, bringing the site to just under 3 million square feet and more than $6 billion invested, along with $1.3 million toward Missouri River valley water-preservation efforts.  
 Then, on May 30, 2025, Google announced an additional $7 billion investment across Iowa over two years, split between a new Cedar Rapids campus and a further expansion of the Council Bluffs facility's cloud and AI infrastructure, atop the ~$6.8 billion already invested in the state. Google states it has now invested more than $20 billion in Iowa since 2007.

name: Tract Altoona
address: Between NE 62nd Avenue and NE 70th Avenue
info: Tract is developing a data center park on a 453-acre, fully entitled site between NE 62nd Avenue and NE 70th Avenue. The developer (via IALCO Polk County LLC) assembled the land in 2025 across three purchases totaling over 500 acres for roughly $20 million, including about $10 million for a ~270-acre parcel, on a site positioned on existing 345kV transmission lines and planned to support over 1 GW of capacity. The Altoona City Council approved annexation and rezoning from agricultural to limited industrial, with 80 acres reserved for future development. Reports of Tract targeting Altoona surfaced in spring 2025, with a formal acquisition announcement in September 2025.

name: COPT Defense Properties Data Center
address: East of Richland Road and north of 360th Street
info: COPT Defense Properties (formerly Corporate Office Properties Trust) acquired a 365-acre parcel in Van Meter, east of Richland Road and north of 360th Street, in late September 2024 for $32 million, announced in October 2024, to expand its data center shell program into the Des Moines market. The site is zoned for data center development and initially planned for about 3.3 million square feet across up to 15 buildings, supported by an estimated 1 gigawatt of power and two substations. The company has indicated the site is intended for an unknown cloud computing tenant and plans to self-fund development in phases.

name: Project Alluvion
info: Project Alluvion is an operational Microsoft data center campus at 550 SE White Crane Road, north of Iowa Highway 5. Announced in April 2014 as a roughly $1.13 billion investment, it was Microsoft's second West Des Moines data center and spans about 127 acres with eight buildings totaling more than 900,000 square feet. The project initially brought about 84 jobs.

name: Project Ginger West
info: Project Ginger West is a Microsoft data center campus under construction at 11100 Booneville Road. Microsoft gained approval for the first two buildings in 2021 and is developing a site planned to total about 1.8 million square feet at an estimated $1 billion investment, with Turner Construction as general contractor. The first building came online in 2023, with full construction estimated for completion by 2028.

name: Project Ginger East
info: Project Ginger East is a Microsoft data center campus under construction at 1475 SE Maffitt Lake Road, east of Veterans Parkway near Microsoft's Alluvion campus. The campus is planned to hold up to five buildings totaling about 1.8 million square feet at an estimated $1 billion investment, with the Weitz Company as general contractor. Building permits were issued in 2024, with construction estimated for completion by 2028.

name: Project Osmium
info: Project Osmium is an operational Microsoft data center campus at 5855 SW Kerry Street, primarily in Warren County, but also with a portion in Madison County. Announced in July 2016 and described at the time as Microsoft's largest U.S. data center deployment, the campus spans roughly 200 acres with a planned total investment of $1.5-2 billion and an estimated 1.7 million square feet across five planned buildings. As of 2024, four buildings were complete with the fifth under construction.

name: PowerHouse Louisville
info: PowerHouse Data Centers and Poe Companies are constructing a ~150-acre hyperscale data center campus on Campground Road.

name: Project Lincoln
info: Western Hospitality Partners originally proposed a $6 billion hyperscale data center campus on 267 acres of agriculturally zoned land outside La Grange. However, as of june 2025, the developer withdrew the proposal and instead submitted a new application for a smaller industrial-zoned site ~3.5 miles away. The new proposal site is located next to the Bluegrass Generating Station.

name: Franklin–Simpson Data Storage and Service Center
address: 421 Steele Road, near Exit 2 of Interstate 65
info: TenKey LandCo is proposing a data center on 200 acres off Steele Road, near Interstate 65. Preliminary plans include three 200,000-square-foot buildings that would be built over three years. In late January 2026, the company has filed a lawsuit against Simpson County Planning and Zoning Commission, claiming they lack the legal authority to use state law to impose operational limits for developments located within city limits.

name: Harrodsburg Data Center
info: JLL is advertising a 500-acre plot of private land off Handy Road near Burgin for a hyperscale data center campus. This is a strategic placement given the site's proximity to the E.W. Brown Generating Station and high-voltage transmission lines. This project is in the early stages and there have been no formal application or rezoning requests submitted yet as of mid February 2026.

name: TeraWulf Hawesville Data Center
info: TeraWulf Inc. acquired the former Century Aluminum smelter site at 1627 State Route 3543—a 750-acre property that idled in 2022 due to soaring energy costs—for $200 million, and is planning to redevelop it into a large-scale AI and HPC data center campus. \n The brownfield site includes more than 250 buildable acres, multiple high-voltage transmission lines, an energized on-site substation, and approximately 480 megawatts of existing power capacity with room to expand. The project has moved quickly: TeraWulf selected Fluor Corporation for master planning and pre-construction services, with a total investment estimated between $3-4 billion and a target completion in the second half of 2027. To fund the buildout, TeraWulf launched an $800 million common stock offering in April 2026, with proceeds directed toward Hawesville construction and repayment of a $500 million bridge credit facility. Locally, TeraWulf has committed to environmental remediation and removal of the old smelter infrastructure, framing the project as one that creates skilled jobs, strengthens the tax base, and reuses existing industrial assets without disturbing new land.

name: Project Pulse
info: A unnamed data center developer under the entity DC Devco LLC was approved to purchase the Southwest Arkansas Mega Site, a total of 991 acres. However, the sale was terminated on May 1, 2026 by the Economic Development Corp. of Clark County after after the developer failed to fulfill a purchase contract, hence, this project is listed as "rejected."

name: Bates Mill No. 3. Data Center
info: Lewiston City officials denied a proposal for a $300-million data center project on the site of Bates Mill No. 3. This project would have allowed for the development of a 85,000-square-foot data center. If the developer appeals this decision, the project will be relisted as delayed.

name: Loring Air Force Base Data Center
info: LiquidCool Solutions is proposing a data center with rentable compute on the site of the former Loring Air Force Base, where 150,000 square feet will be leased from Green 4 Maine’s innovation hub. This project is unique in that it would not consume water, and instead uses polyalphaolefin oil to cool equipment.

name: Millinocket Data Center
address: Former Great Northern Paper Company site
info: Nautilus Data Technologies withdrew a proposal for a $300-million data center campus on the site of the former Great Northern Paper Company site because the developer was unable to secure a tenant due to insufficient power generation.

name: Sanford Woods Industrial and Technical Campus
info: A developer is proposing an approximately $2.16 billion technical, agricultural, and industrial campus on 1,060 acres along the Mousam River, including a data center. According to the proposal, the campus would feature an AI data center, a cold storage facility, and a large greenhouse complex. The campus would initially be powered by natural gas, with plans to transition to hydrogen fuel cells within five years. Estimated water consumption is 300,000 to 500,000 gallons per day.

name: Woodlawn Data Center
info: Security Land and Development LP, is proposing a 150-MW, 42-acre data center campus. Located near the Social Security Administration Headquarters, this project includes the construction of a new BGE electrical substation. Construction could begin as early as mid-2026.

name: Brightseat Tech Park Data Center Development
info: Lerner Enterprises is proposing a $5-billion, ~4.1-million-square-foot data center campus, dubbed the Brightseat Technology Park, on the former Landover Mall site. The project is still in the review phase

name: Laurel Data Center
companies: TPC Data Center via Mid-Atlantic LLC
info: TPC Data Center has proposed a 20-MW data center project on a ~7.56 acre site next to the Burtonsville Substation in Laurel.

name: Federick County Zoning Overlay + Quantum Frederick Park Data Center Campus
address: 5601 Manor Woods Rd, former Alcoa site
companies: Rowan Digital Infrastructure; TPG Real Estate
info: Data center development in Frederick County is now concentrated within a special zoning overlay of roughly 2,600 acres surrounding the former Eastalco aluminum plant north of Adamstown. The County’s zoning amendments restrict data centers to less than 1% of total land area, require buffers from residential neighborhoods and schools, and mandate farmland preservation at a five-to-one ratio for new development. Several sites within the Quantum Frederick Campus have already broken ground, including Rowan’s Bauxite. The overlay was adopted to allow large-scale data center growth in a single designated zone while preventing broader industrial expansion across rural areas of the county.

name: Piney Reach Data Center Development
address: Billingsley Road, near Charles County Landfill and recycling center
info: NAI Michael is advertising a ~432 (total) acre site for data center development on Billingsley Road.

name: Johns Hopkins Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Institute (DSAI)
address: Intersection of Wyman Park Drive and Remington Avenue.
info: Johns Hopkins University is constructing two new buildings for its Data Science and AI Institute, an advanced research center focused on artificial intelligence (AI), high-performance computing (HPC), and data science. The facilities will be located on the Homewood Campus adjacent to the Wyman Building and the SNF Agora Institute. The project will expand the university’s capacity for interdisciplinary research and collaboration, and the buildings will include classrooms, laboratories, faculty offices, and collaborative workspaces. Construction is expected to begin in April 2026 and continue through 2029. In May 2026, the Maryland Board of Public Works on approved a $9 million grant for this project to support life sciences and biomedical research, as well as physics, public health, engineering, and astronomy.

name: Atmosphere Dickerson Data Center
info: Atmosphere Data Centers is proposing a 360 MW, Tier III+, 5-building data center complex on 170 acres zoned for heavy industrial development at the former Dickerson coal plant. According to Atmosphere's official site, the data center will only encompass ~110 acres. The property owner, Terra Energy, is also constructing a 2,000 MWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) adjacent to the site.

name: Westfield Data Center Campus
info: Servistar Realties is proposing a $4-billion data center campus in Westfield that would make it the largest data center campus in the state of Massachusetts. The 10-building, 2.7 million-square-foot project on Servistar Industrial Way aims to support AI and cloud services, utilizing state tax incentives to become a major regional tech hub. The project is expected to create 400 full-time jobs and is expected to break ground in 2026.

name: Project Mitten / The Barn
address: 11600 W Michigan Ave, East of the Rustic Glen Golf Club
info: OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital are collaborating on a $7 billion data center complex east of the Rustic Glen Golf Club, as a extension of their Stargate Project. This 2.2-million-square-foot data center would be Michigan's first hyperscale data center. Oracle states that the campus is being developed responsibly, with the footprint being only 250 of 575 total acres. According to the "Michigan Courts" document linked, the Township acknowledges that the developer/tenant intends to pursue a sales and use tax exemption for the project as a qualified data center under the terms of the Michigan General Sales Tax Act, MCL 205.54ee (the “Use Tax Exemption”).

name: Project Cannoli
address: Near I-94, between Haggerty Rd and Hannan Rd
info: Panattoni Data Center Group is proposing a 800,000-square-foot data center campus on a 280-acre site, with three data processing buildings, an office and water services building, and a substation. This project is expected to have a capacity of 1 GW and will use from 2-3.6 million gallons of water a day.

name: Project Splitrock
info: Randee LLC is proposing a hyperscale data center campus on 1077 acres of land north of I-96. In September 2025, the Howell Township Planning Commission voted unanimously against rezoning the land for data centers. In December, the developer Randee LLC withdrew the rezone request. Simultaneously, the township enacted a six-month moratorium on data center development.

name: 32 Mile Road Technical Campus
address: South side of 32 Mile Road between M-53 and Powell Road
info: Prologis submitted a rezoning request for approximately 312 acres across four parcels to develop a data center campus on the south side of 32 Mile Road between M-53 and Powell Road. A decision on the request has been repeatedly postponed while the township considers implementing a zoning ordinance, with the next meeting scheduled for June 11, 2026.

name: Benton Charter Township Data Center
address: 2274 Yore Avenue, nearby Lake Michigan College
info: An undisclosed developer is proposing a $3 billion data center campus on 280 acres near Lake Michigan College. Not much information has been disclosed about this proposal, but it is suspected that this data center would source power from nearby nuclear plants. Activity for this proposal has been inactive since 2024, hence this entry is listed as delayed.

name: Microsoft Gaines Township Data Center
address: Along 76th St and Patterson Ave intersection; 7174 Patterson Ave
info: Microsoft is currently in the process of purchasing homes in the area for the campus, which according to the proposed site plan, features 7 buildings.

name: Rocklocker Kalkaska Data Center
address: Near Island Lake Rd and South River Rd
info: Rocklocker withdrew plans for a proposed 1,440 acre data center campus on public Department of Natural Resources land due to community pushback in November 2025.

name: Ypsilanti University of Michigan × Los Alamos Supercomputing Research Center
address: Intersection of Textile Road and Bridge Road, former South Hydro Park and Norfolk Southern Railway Holding properties.
info: The University of Michigan, in collaboration with Los Alamos National Laboratory, is proposing a $1.25 billion, 124-acre AI/HPC supercomputing facility at the intersection of Textile Road and Bridge Road. The campus will include a federal research facility, an academic research facility, and a new dedicated substation to exclusively power the facility. Construction is anticipated to begin in 2027 and continue through 2031. According to the University of Michigan, this project is expected to create over 200 permanent high-paying jobs in research and technical operations, 30-50 staff positions to run and maintain the computing network, and about 300 construction jobs with union labor.

name: Project Cherry Blossom
address: On the former ProMedica Hospital site off I-75 and North Dixie Highway
info: Cloverleaf Infrastructure is proposing a data center campus on 200 acres on a former ProMedica Hospital site.

name: Project Ironwood
address: Intersection of Ann Arbor Rd and Day Rd
info: Cloverleaf Infrastructure is proposing a data center on 750 acres at the corner of Ann Arbor Road and Day Road.

name: Project Flex
address: Several parcels between Milford Rd & South Hill Rd (South Hill Business Park West)
info: Verrus is proposing a 172-acre data center campus composed of six buildings totaling approximately 1.8 million square feet of floor area and a colocated substation. This project is anticipated to create 210 permanent full-time jobs once operational.

name: Deep Green Lansing Data Center
address: South Cedar Street and East Kalamazoo Street
info: Deep Green, a UK based data center company, has purchased a 3 acre parking lot in downtown Lansing, MI. The company has pledged to donate the 290,000 BTU of annual heat generated by the 24 MW data center to the city's downtown hot water system, the first of its kind in the United States. The site will use 500,000 gallons of water per year for its closed loop cooling system. The site will generate its own energy, but the type has not been specified. The project was proposed late 2025. As of April 2026, Deep Green withdrew its application for a zoning amendment that would have made the project possible. The reason for withdrawal was not made clear.

name: Pavilion Township Data Center
address: South 26th Street and East N Avenue
info: Franklin Partners is proposing a data center on farmland at the intersection of South 26th Street and East N Avenue. This proposal remains paused, largely due to community opposition, zoning issues, and concerns about energy and water consumption.

name: Sansone York Township Data Center
address: Across from Toyota Motor North American R&D Headquarters, between E Willis Rd, Platt Rd, and Bemis Rd
info: Sansone Group, a St. Louis-based real estate developer, is exploring the purchase of ~412 acres owned by Toyota for a potential hyperscale data center campus. Located between Platt Road and Willis Road, this facility would supposedly have a capacity of 1 GW. As of early 2026, no formal development application has been submitted to local authorities, and discussions about the project remain preliminary.

name: Metrobloks Southfield Data Center
address: Along Inkster Road, south of I-696
info: Metrobloks is proposing a two-story, 109,683-square-foot data center on approximately 12 acres of vacant land in Southfield. This proposal utilizes closed-loop cooling and involves the construction of a new substation for powering the facility, at the developers expense. This proposal is significantly smaller than many data center projects proposed elsewhere in Michigan.

name: Clinton Amazon Data Center
info: Amazon's Clinton data center project, announced in April 2026, retrofits the former Delphi Corporation and Milwaukee Tool plant and represents Amazon's first location in the city, with a planned investment of $1 billion and the creation of 100 jobs. Amazon has mentioned that the Clinton facility will use no water for cooling operations. According to WLBT, on January 15th, Clinton and Hinds County both held special meetings to discuss a FILOT agreement with an unnamed entity. Almost a month later, Amazon Data Services acquired the property. On March 2nd, city documents were found tying the tax deal to a data center.

name: Ridgeland Amazon Data Center
address: Corner of Highland Colony Parkway and West County Line Road
info: Governor Tate Reeves announced in April 2026 that Amazon will invest an additional $11 billion to expand its existing data center operations in Madison County, with one of the new facilities located in Ridgeland. The Ridgeland site is located on the Costas Property, west of Tougaloo College near County Line Road. The Ridgeland campus is expected to use groundwater only about 30 days of the year, or roughly 9% of the time.

name: Canton Amazon Data Center
info: Amazon Web Services is investing $10 billion to build a hyperscale data center complex outside of Canton at the Madison County Mega Site, spanning part of roughly 1,700 acres across two sites. Amazon broke ground on this campus two years ago, and it was the largest capital investment in Mississippi's history at the time. Amazon has pledged to use 100% recycled water to cool the servers at its Canton data centers.

name: Vicksburg Amazon Data Center
info: Amazon announced in November 2025 a $3 billion AI data center off U.S. 61 South Warren, with construction set to begin in 2026, along with a $150,000 educational grant for Warren County. The facility is expected to create at least 200 high-paying full-time jobs and falls under Mississippi's 2024 Major Economic Impact Authority legislation that has supported Amazon's broader data center expansion in the state.

name: Project Taurus
info: AVAIO Digital Partners is planning a $6 billion data center campus, nicknamed Project Taurus, on 175 acres in the East Metropolitan Center Business Park. Announced in Aug 2025, the AI-ready campus will support cloud and AI workloads with sustainable design (rainwater capture, solar, efficient cooling). The project will be powered by Entergy and have multi-hundred-megawatt capacity (~450 MW online by 2027). Initial phases will create about 60 direct high-tech jobs (plus hundreds of construction and support jobs).

name: Meridian Compass Data Center
info: The Compass Datacenters campus being constructed in Meridian is a $10 billion hyperscale project comprising eight data center buildings, each 250,000 square feet with 40 MW of IT capacity, to be built over eight years in Lauderdale County. The official groundbreaking was held on January 28, 2025, with the first data center anticipated to be completed by 2026, and the facility will be powered by approximately 500 MW from Mississippi Power. The project is expected to create thousands of jobs and, alongside a separate $10 billion Amazon Web Services campus in Madison County, represents one of the largest economic development investments in Mississippi's history.

name: Clarksdale Data Center
address: Adjacent Hwy 61 and the Sunflower Ave
info: In March 2026, Clarksdale officials voted to approve a rezone with conditions to allow for a data center on a 648-acre site. The developer would be required to pay one-time $5-million impact fee prior to any work.

name: xAI MACROHARDRR
info: xAI is investing over $20 billion to build a major data center in Southaven, Mississippi, known as MACROHARDRR, a playful jab at rival Microsoft. The 800,000-square-foot facility sits in DeSoto County adjacent to the Tennessee border, forming part of a tri-center AI cluster that xAI CFO Anthony Armstrong described as housing "the world's largest supercomputer" with a combined 2 gigawatts of computing power. Governor Tate Reeves called it the largest private investment in the state's history, with the project expected to bring hundreds of permanent jobs and significant tax revenue to DeSoto County. Despite the enthusiasm, the NAACP and the Southern Environmental Law Center have raised concerns over air pollution near predominantly Black communities in Memphis, and a local advocacy group submitted a petition citing concerns about noise and emissions from the gas turbines powering the site.

name: Nebius Independence Hyperscale Campus
address: Eastgate Commerce Center near Little Blue Parkway and Highway 78
info: Nebius is planning a roughly $6.6 billion hyperscale AI data center campus on roughly 398 acres. IT capacity is expected be 800 MW to 1.2 GW with up to 10 buildings. Project power is tied to the reopened Blue Valley Power Plant. According to Nebius, the project will create approximately 1200 construction jobs and 130 permanent high-tech positions at full operation.

name: Project Green
info: AWS's 'Project Green' via NorthPoint Development is a roughly 1,000-acre hyperscale campus near New Florence with up to 21 buildings and minimum investment cited around $8.5 billion.

name: Project Spade
info: Google is developing 'Project Spade' via Related Digital, which is the second New Florence-area hyperscale campus, described as about 780 plus 130 acres with site plan approval in November 2025. According to Related Digital, the facility would deliver 1.2 GW of utility capacity.

name: Crusoe Warrenton AI Data Center
info: Crusoe received approval for their conditional use application to construct a data center campus off Route 70. Plans feature 2 data center buildings, each around 800,000 square feet, along with an electric substation, a switchyard, and an administration building.

name: Wildwood Ranch AI Data Center
address: Intersection of Central City Road and West 20th Street
info: Wildwood Ranch LLC is proposing an AI data center on the Wildwood Ranch site after 540 acres were annexed and more than 600 acres rezoned to M-2. Potential capacity is described as at least 200 MW. An end user/tenant has not been not disclosed.

name: CRG Festus Data Center
info: CRG, the real estate development arm of Clayco, is moving forward with a controversial $6 billion, ~360-acre hyperscale data center project in Festus, despite intense local opposition and a pending lawsuit from residents. The project, located near Highway 67, was approved by the city council in March 2026, leading to a complete turnover of local leadership in subsequent elections.

name: Armory Innovation Data Center
companies: Contour; TeraWatt; THO; Steadfast; ARCO; Lewis Rice
info: Armory Innovation Data Center is a 525,000-square-foot, 120 MW, approximately $3B project near the Armory in St. Louis. City permit approval/final approval was reported in April 2026.

name: Mo Lake Development Cross Creek Data Center
address: Unknown, somewhere north of Camdenton at the planned development
info: Proposed roughly 500,000-square-foot data center at Cross Creek Subdivision near Camdenton. Camden County rejected the Opportunity Zone 3-0 in April 2025, and Camdenton later passed a one-year moratorium.

name: Metrobloks Liberty Data Center Campus
info: Metrobloks announced a $1.4B AI-ready data center campus at 2515 Old Hughes Road, planned for 568,800 square feet across three buildings and about 150 MW. According to Metrobloks, the site is approximately 30 acres in total.

name: White Cloud Acres
info: Scale Microgrids is proposing AI data center and off-grid natural gas power plant somewhere in southern Nodaway County near Maryville, representing an approximately $6 billion investment in total.

name: Lambda AI Factory
info: Lambda, the Superintelligence Cloud, announced in October 2025 that they are planning to transform an unoccupied 2009 facility into an AI factory in Kansas City. The site is expected to launch in early 2026 with 24MW of capacity, and the potential to scale up to more than 100MW in the future.

name: Jasper County Powered Data Park / Sarcoxie Data Center
address: Between County Road 20 and County Road 30
info: Geronimo Power proposed a hyperscale data center with co-located solar farm and battery park in eastern Jasper County south of Highway 96 and west of La Russell.

name: Oppidan Foristell Data Center Sites
info: Oppidan Investment Company and its subsidiary, Connect, are pursuing two potential data center sites totaling more than 400 acres along I-70. After resident opposition, annexation was amended to retain agricultural zoning.

name: Meramec Valley Technology Park
info: The Meramec Valley Technology Park is a proposed 500-acre, 16-building data center campus in rural Franklin County, near Pacific. Developed by Logistix, the $16 billion project includes a massive data center complex and a new natural gas-fired power plant. It faces significant resident opposition over noise, environmental, and infrastructure concerns. Visuals of the site plan include a new substation and multiple stormwater detention ponds.

name: Gateway Digital Campus
address: Near the intersection of MO-100 and I-44
info: Provident Data Centers' Gateway Digital Campus, to be located at Diamond Farms, is a 13-building hyperscale proposal on approximately 575 acres at MO-100/I-44 near Gray Summit. Franklin County Planning & Zoning recommended approval by a 5-4 vote on April 21, 2026.

name: Meta Kansas City Data Center
address: Along NW 128 Street and Arrowhead Trafficway
info: Meta's Kansas City Data Center at Diode Ventures' Golden Plains Technology Park near I-435/US-169. The source row describes the campus as operational plus under construction.

name: Project Mica
address: Northeast corner of Interstate 435 and Highway 169
info: Google's Project Mica is a planned/active $10B data center campus near Smithville/Kansas City, described as roughly 500 acres, five buildings, and about 700 MW.

name: Marshfield Webster Data Center
info: Lumon Solutions / Lumon Solutions Marshfield, LLC, a company local to Webster County, is building a small-scale high-performance computing (HPC) facility on approximately five acres on Rifle Range Road north of Marshfield near a substation. According to a developer associated with the project, the current plan consists of several small modular buildings totaling approximately 7,000 square feet, as opposed to large hyperscale data center campuses that can reach over 1 million square feet.

name: Project Cumulus
address: Near Highway 370, between Huster Road and Harry S Truman Boulevard
info: Project Cumulus was a hyperscale data center proposal by CRG, the development arm of construction giant Clayco, for an unnamed Fortune 100 customer on a 440-acre site off Highway 370 in St. Charles. On August 18, 2025, CRG notified the city it was withdrawing its conditional use permit application, pulling the project from the August 19 City Council agenda. Notably, CRG framed the withdrawal as temporary, stating its intent to return with a revised proposal rather than abandon the project outright. Only a few days later, the St. Charles City Council unanimously passed a one-year moratorium on new data center applications, making St. Charles the first city in the nation to enact a citywide ban on data center construction.

name: DataOne Vineland
companies: DataOne; Ardian; BSO; DataOne; Nebius Group; Microsoft; Vineland Municipal Utilities; Atlantic City Electric
info: DataOne, a French company backed by infrastructure investor Ardian and launched through global connectivity firm BSO, is constructing a large-scale AI data center in Vineland, in partnership with Amsterdam-based Nebius Group. The facility, located on South Lincoln Avenue off Route 55, was originally planned at 2.4 million square feet but has since been scaled back to under 718,000 square feet. It is designed to reach 300 megawatts of capacity, generating 85% of its power on-site via natural gas with the remaining 15% drawn from Vineland Municipal Utilities and Atlantic City Electric. \n In January 2026, the Vineland City Council voted unanimously to approve a PILOT (Payment in Lieu of Taxes) agreement, giving DataOne tax breaks in exchange for direct payments to the city; DataOne separately declined a $6.2 million city loan following resident opposition. The company has promised more than 200 permanent jobs and plans to operate an on-site vertical farm that would use the facility's heat and CO₂ waste to provide produce to low-income residents. Despite these commitments, the project has drawn sustained community pushback — including a protest of roughly 100 residents in March 2026 — over concerns about emissions, construction noise, and a lack of transparency. Construction is ongoing, with a target completion date of November 2026.

name: Princeton Nurseries Data Center
address: Former Princeton Nurseries site, off Route 1
info: WRV Nurseries South Brunswick LLC has proposed a 160-acre data center project on the former Princeton Nurseries site.

name: Nutley Data Center
info: Prism Capital Partners has been granted approval to redevelop the former Hoffmann-La Roche site into a data center.

name: New Brunswick Data Center
info: Developers proposed a small 27,000-square-foot data center at a blighted site at 100 Jersey Ave. As of mid-February, the New Brunswick City Council has voted against the project, and instead plan on building a park in its place.

name: East Greenwich Township Data Center
info: American Tower is proposing an 17,000-square-foot “unmanned data storage center” on residentially zoned land along Mantua Road, adjacent to the Oak Ridge housing development. In June 20-26, the developer withdrew the project application.

name: CoreWeave Kenilworth
info: CoreWeave is constructing a $1.8 billion AI data center across 392,600 square feet at the former Merck & Co. campus. This project was the first granted a $250 million tax credit award under the Next New Jersey Program – AI (NJEDA), which is aimed at drawing significant capital investment, creating high-paying jobs and cementing New Jersey’s position as a leader in the AI industry economy, according to the EDA. Construction began in late 2025, and operation is anticipated in early 2027.

name: Millville Energy & Data Center Campus
info: A proposal for a 2.6 million-square-foot, 1.4GW (scaled) hyperscale AI and HPC data center campus was rejected by Millville Commissioners on May 19 via a data center ban. According to the official project website, the campus would have featured five data center buildings, an on-site existing substation, and a "gas house."

name: Monroe Township Data Center
info: Monroe Township in Gloucester County, New Jersey has effectively blocked a proposed data center after its planning board denied an application from Hexa Builders for a project on 172 acres of vacant farmland. The proposal would have included a 1 million-square-foot data center alongside a 522,000-square-foot warehouse, but was rejected on the grounds that the application was "incomplete." \n The township had already passed a ban on data centers in April 2026, driven by resident concerns over electricity and water consumption, noise, and general quality-of-life impacts. Hexa's application had to be considered regardless because it predated the ban, but with its denial, the ban is now fully in effect.

name: STAMP Data Center
info: Stream Data Centers, a Texas-based company, has proposed a 2.2-million-square-foot multi-billion-dollar data center at the STAMP site. The project is expected to create more than 120 permanent jobs plus hundreds of construction jobs, with groundbreaking targeted for 2026 and full operations projected by 2030.

name: Cayuga Data Campus
address: Former Cayuga Power Plant site, Lansing
info: TeraWulf is redeveloping the Miliken Station at the former Cayuga Power Plant site into a 180-acre data center campus. The campus will have an initial ~50 MW potential, scalable up to ~138 MW.

name: Lysander Data Center
info: Ranalli Super DC, LLC is proposing a large-scale hyperscale data center campus in Lysander. The project would be built on ~124 acres of land and has requested up to 300 megawatts of grid interconnection capacity, with development currently in the early planning stage.

name: Niagara Digital Campus Data Center Development
address: Somewhere near the intersection of Rainbow and John B. Daly Boulevards
info: Niagara Falls Redevelopment is proposing a ~$1.5B data center and technology campus.

name: Oneonta West End Data Center
info: Startup EcoYotta filed zoning amendment application to rezone ~153 acres from residential-ag agricultural to planned development district for a proposed data center.

name: Brookhaven National Laboratory Data Center
address: Adjacent to William Floyd Parkway, on Brookhaven National Labratory's campus
info: The U.S. Department of Energy is considering developing an AI data center on a 90-acre section of Brookhaven National Laboratory's campus, adjacent to the William Floyd Parkway. The proposed site is nearby the Caithness Long Island Energy Center, a 350 MW gas plant.

name: Sugar Hill Portland Data Center
address: Former Sugar Hill Golf Course, off Route 5
info: WEED Inc., a cannabis and hemp bioresearch company, received general support from the Portland, New York Town Board to convert the former Sugar Hill Golf Course into a data center campus. The roughly 44-acre property along Route 5 near the Lake Erie shoreline in Chautauqua County is owned by WEED Inc.'s subsidiary, The Four Winds of Lake Erie, LLC. The board's resolution initiates further due diligence, including environmental review and regulatory approvals, so there has not been a final green light. The project faces significant community opposition.

name: Massena Petawatt Data Center Site Advertisement
address: Around the intersection of Route 43 and Horton Road
info: Five 9s digital is advertising a 200 MW data center development site encompassing approximately 140 acre site across two parcels off Horton Road and Route 42, near the Dennison Substation. According to the advertisement, this land is a pre-approved 200 MW land site.

name: Ilion Data Center
info: Turin Hoefler Avenue LLC has submitted an application to remediate a ~7.84 acre parcel ("Site C") to construct a 250,000-square-foot data center at the former Remington Arms site at 14 Hoefler Avenue.

name: Riverview Innovative and Technology Campus Data Center
info: A 300MW data center development is proposed for a 140-acre former Tonawanda Coke Corporation (TCC) plant. The site, previously home to the TCC since 1917, closed around 2018 following TCC's bankruptcy. Developers Jon Williams and John Yensan purchased it in 2019 for just $1 at auction, rebranding it the Riverview Innovation and Technology Campus. The proposed facility would span 500,000 sq ft and is currently before the Town of Tonawanda planning board, with cleanup and demolition of the old coke plant still underway.

name: Rural Hall Data Center
companies: Thomas & Hutton; Drox Group LLC; Montrose Environmental Group
info: A data center is being proposed on a 130 acre plot of land that used to be the Wall Farm in Rural Hall. The site is planned to have four buildings around 78 ft. tall. The town hall cannot vote on the matter because the land falls outside of their jurisdiction to the Winston-Salem/Forsyth County Planning Board. They are set to meet on May 9th to take on the matter and vote on a zoning ordinance to turn the residential (RS9 and RS20) zoned farm to light industrial (LI-S). The proposal has been met with opposition from local residents.

name: Person County Mega Park
companies: Microsoft Corporation; Timmons Group; Microsoft Corporation; Centurylink; Spectrum; Person County Mega Park; Duke Energy; Dominion Energy
info: Microsoft purchased 1,350 acres of land in October 2024 for $17.15 million from the Person County Economic Development Commission to build both a chip manufacturing facility and a data center. The data center takes up 175 acres of this land. Electricity will be provided by Duke Energy, and Peason County, with grants from NC Commerce, have built infrastrucutre to deliver 2 million gallons of water per day to the facility. This site has favorable zoning as it sits within a Federal Opportunity Zone. Separately for the manufacturing plant, the site is within Foreign Trade Zone 93, which can help lower import taxes. U.S. Army Corps of Engineers  and the North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality Division of Water Resources have issued permits for the redirection of streams and wetlands and for filling the land for development. These permits last 10 years and will expire on December 31, 2030.

name: Tarboro Data Center Campus
companies: ARCO Design/Build; 142 Design Group; Energy Storage Solutions
info: Energy Storage Systems plans to build a data center on 52 acres of land currently owned by Edgewater County in Tarboro. Although the size of the land is smaller than a typical project at comparable power consumption, the developer still claims they will provide their own energy in the form of a 300 MW natural gas plant. The project will ultimately cost $6.4 billion as stated in the official plans. The data center will also include a 300 MW/1200MWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) for the site. The Tarboro Town Council voted 7-1 against the request for a Special Use Permit 25-02 to construct the data center on the land. EES has appealed to the superior court of Edgecomb county.

name: Fayetteville Data Center Campus
companies: Energy Storage Solutions (ESS); PWC (Fayetteville Public Works Commission); Duke Energy

name: Lenoir Data Center Expansion
address: 708 Lynhaven Drive, and 914 Virginia St + 919 Fairview Dr parcels
companies: Google; Tapaha Dynamics LLC; Google; Blue Ridge Energy

name: Rowan County Data Center
address: Long Ferry Road (near Duke Energy Buck Steam Station)
companies: Edged (EDC Charlotte LLC); Edged (EDC Charlotte LLC); Duke Energy

name: Project Delta
companies: Engineered Land Solutions (ELS); DFC Stokes LLC; Blue Ridge Companies; CleanAIRE NC; Dan River Basin Association; National Hairston Clan; David Couch / Blue Ridge Companies; Duke Energy (Belews Creek Steam Station)

name: AWS Richmond County Campus
companies: Amazon Web Services; Amazon Web Services; Duke Energy (Sherwood H. Smith Jr. Energy Complex)

name: Statesville Data Center Campus
address: Stamey Farm Road / Hickory Highway, off I-40
companies: Compass Datacenters; Brookfield Infrastructure (owner of Compass); Compass Datacenters; Duke Energy

name: CLT20 Moores Chapel
companies: Digital Realty Trust; Digital Moores Chapel LLC; Digital Realty Trust; Digital Realty Trust

name: CLT10 Uptown Expansion
companies: Digital Realty Trust; Digital Realty Trust; Digital Realty Trust

name: Charlotte University City Data Center
companies: PowerHouse Data Centers (AREP); Town Lane; American Real Estate Partners (AREP); Town Lane; PowerHouse Data Centers; PowerHouse Data Centers; Town Lane; Duke Energy

name: 5C Charlotte Data Center
companies: 5C Group; Hypertec Cloud; 5C Data Centers; Brookfield Asset Management; Deutsche Bank; 5C Group

name: NC-1 Campus (Madison) Data Center
companies: WhiteFiber HPC; Bit Digital; Enovum Data Centers; Bit Digital; WhiteFiber HPC; WhiteFiber HPC; Nscale; Rockingham County

name: Microsoft — Catawba County Campus Cluster (Lyle Creek / Boyd Farm / Hickory / Stover North)
companies: Whiting-Turner Contracting; Morgan Construction; Ames Construction; Microsoft Corporation; Whiting-Turner Contracting; Morgan Construction; Ames Construction; Microsoft Corporation; Duke Energy

name: Greensboro Dream Center
address: 2901 E. Gate City Blvd (Gateway Research Park)
companies: ImpactData; Raeden; ImpactData; Raeden; Gateway Research Park; NC A&T State University

name: Marble Data Center Campus
companies: Core Scientific; CoreWeave; Core Scientific; CoreWeave

name: Raleigh New Build Data Center
companies: Flexential; GI Partners; Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners; Flexential

name: Maiden iDataCenter Campus
info: Apple is investing $175 million into a 237,600 square-foot data center on its existing campus on Startown Road. Apple is also constructing a 20 MW solar power facility to support its operations, making it the largest solar farm dedicated to data centers.

name: T5 Data Centers Kings Mountain
companies: T5 Data Centers; T5 Data Centers; AT&T; Walt Disney Co.; Wipro; Duke Energy
info: T5 Data Centers is expanding their existing colocation campus (?)

name: American Tower RA1
address: At ATC cellular tower site (Research Triangle region)

name: Lenoir Campus (Existing + Project Cardinal 2024)
companies: Google; Tapaha Dynamics LLC; Blue Ridge Energy

name: Natelli Investments New Hill Digital Campus
companies: Natelli Investments LLC; Protect Wake County Coalition; Natelli Investments LLC
info: Natelli Investments has withdrawn a proposal for a 250 MW data center adjacent to the Shearon Harris Nuclear Plant.

name: Tract Mooresville Technology Park
address: Between Patterson Farm Road and Rustic Road

name: Project Accelerate
companies: East John Master Development; Matthews DC LLC; Engineered Land Solutions (ELS); East John Master Development

name: Kingsboro Data Center
companies: ARCO Design/Build; 142 Design Group; Energy Storage Solutions
info: Energy Storage Systems plans to build a data center on 121 acres of land currently owned by Edgewater County in Kingsboro, outside Rocky Mount. The developer claims they will provide their own energy in the form of a 300 MW natural gas plant. The company's president told local news in December that it will need up to 900 MW and cost $19.2 billion. The data center will also include a 300 MW/1200MWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) for the site.

name: Vantage New Albany Data Center Campus
info: Vantage Data Centers is currently constructing a 70-acre, $2 billion hyperscale data center campus outside New Albany, their first in the midwest. Comprised of three two-story buildings across 1.5-million square feet, the campus will draw up to 192 MW of power, and is being built in phases.

name: AWS Innovation Campus / New Albany Data Center Campus
info: AWS is developing a massive $3.5 billion, 1.25 million-square-foot, five-building data center campus on 85 acres along Beech and Miller Roads in New Albany, Ohio, with initial buildings operational as of August 2025.

name: QTS New Albany Data Center Campus
info: QTS has approval to make site-specific improvements to their existing 612,000-square-foot, data centers at their New Albany campus.

name: AWS Hillard Campus
info: The Amazon Web Service campus in Hillard is expanding its current capacity from 382 MW to rouhly 401 MW through its deal with BloomEnergy to build 228 natural gas fuel cells on the property. This project was originally set to be 7 buildings on the 141 acre plot, but has since been decreased to 5 in favor of these fuel cells. Buildings A-C have been completed and D and E remain under construction. This site is also colocated with an AEP (American Electric Power Inc.) owned and operated substation that supplies electricity to the grid. However, the new fuel cells will be owned and operated solely by AWS and there are currently no plans to connect it to the grid.

name: Project Mila / Trenton Industrial Park Data Center
address: Off Woodside and Kennel Rd, Trenton Industrial Park
info: Prologis is proposing a $7.75 million, 1-million-square-foot data center on 141 acres at the Trenton Industrial Park.

name: Hamilton Data Center
info: Logistix Property Group is proposing a 320,000-square-foot data center on ~29.2 acres along University Blvd.

name: 5C Springfield Data Center
info: 5C Data Center is developing a massive $1.3 billion, 214,000-square-foot AI hyperscale data center campus at the site of the former LexisNexis data center. This project is supported by a 15-year enterprise zone tax abatement and is expected to reach full operational capacity in 2026/2027. Note that the map boundary/area represents the entire parcel, and the data center footprint is smaller.

name: Ashville Data Center
address: North of State Route 752 toward Weigand Road
info: EdgeConneX is proposing a data center campus on a 680-acre site in Ashville. This proposal involved annexing a portion of another town into Ashville to create a single contiguous data center campus. However, in early February 2026, the Ashville Village Council rejected the annexation, restricting EdgeConneX to a 200 acre plot.

name: Piqua Data Center
address: Piqua I-75 Business and Industrial Park
info: A developer has proposed a hyperscale data center campus worth over $1 billion on a 1168-acre site off I-75.

name: Project Galaxy
address: North side of Millcreek Road, west of Vandemark Road.
info: AWS is constructing a $3 billion hyperscale data center campus on Millcreek Road. Construction is set to begin in January 2026, with completion expected by 2030.

name: Google Franklin Furnace Data Center
info: Google is proposing a $1 billion data center campus featuring a 500,000-square foot data center in the Haverhill area. This project received approval by county commissioners for a 15-year, 75% property tax abatement tied to a payment-in-lieu-of-taxes agreement starting at $500,000 annually.

name: Van Wert Mega Site
address: Along US 30 between Marsh Rd and Gilliland Rd
info: Thor Equities has purchased a 1475 acre site for $75 million and plans to build a 500MW data center.

name: Stargate Project - Lordstown AI Data Center Campus
info: OpenAI, in partnership with SoftBank and Oracle, is transforming a former GM/Foxconn factory in Lordstown into a major hub for the $500 billion Stargate AI project.

name: Lordstown Data Center
address: Off Interstate 80 and Tod Ave SW
info: Bristolville 25 Developer LLC is proposing a $3.6 billion, 1.65 million square-foot AI data center on 133 across both Trumbull and Mahoning, adjacent to one of Project Stargate's data center complexes. The developer states that the project would include $10.8 million to update Lordstown’s water system, a community fund, the employment of 1,600 construction laborers and 120 full-time workers at the center, and tax revenue. Despite this, the Lordstown Council voted to ban data centers, and then reversed their decision to develop a moratorium on data centers for 180 days instead. Due to this, the developer filed a lawsuit against the town,

name: Bitdeer Weathersfield Project
info: BitDeer has purchased a 41.8 acre site for $3.4 million that includes an interconnection agreement with FirstEnergy to supply power to the 300 MW site. The site, previously the Niles Trumbull power station, will mainly mine cryptocurrencies for the Singapore-based company.

name: Cipher Mining's "Ulysses"
info: Cipher Mining acquired a 200 MW, 195-acre site named "Ulysses" in Ohio in December 2025, marking its first expansion outside Texas to diversify into high-performance computing (HPC) and AI workloads. The site, which connects to the PJM market, is expected to be energized in late 2027. This is data center marks Cipher Mining's first expansion outside Texas.

name: Google Lockbourne
info: Google has developed a $300 million data center campus in Lockbourne, located near 5076 S High. The campus features multiple data center buildings constructed on the site of the former Hartman Farm, with development supported by approximately $54.3 million in local tax incentives approved in 2021.

name: Johnstown Cologix Data Center
address: Off Duncan Plains Rd NW, Johnstown
info: Cologix, a Denver based company, plans to build approximately across 2 million square feet data centers, eight buildings in total, on a 154 acre plot of land in Johnstown. This is the 7th data center operated by Cologix in the Colombus area.

name: Project Cosmos
info: Amazon is investing $1 billion in the construction of Project Cosmos at 33 Innovation Park in Marysville. The newest facility on the site is expected to open in 2027 and create at least 25 jobs. The current parcel is owned by the City of Marysville, and reports from a school board meeting shows renderings of the plot, confirming the location and providing acrage estimates. This site might be a part of Marysville South, another project across Industrial Parkway with AWS as its end user, that reports the same investment, timeline, and employment numbers.

name: Cologix Lewis Center Data Center
