This represents a $152.1 million investment and has led the developer to seek a "15-year payment-in-lieu-of-taxes (pilot) with the Economic Development Growth Engine for Memphis and Shelby County (EDGE). The project is expected to be complete in 2027. IT load will be scaled across phases, starting at 15 MW, then 20 MW (2025), and finally 60 MW (2027).

name: Lawrenceburg / Ethridge Data Center
type: Rezone Approved, Data Center Developer Affiliated
info: The City of Lawrenceburg posted a public notice in late December 2025 of a proposed rezoning for a plot of land off for a 60,000-square-foot data center on 6.5 acres of a ~42-acre parcel off Flatwoods Road. The associated applicant is listed as Cadence, LLC, and is a registered agent for the property owners. The land was successfully rezoned on January 8th from large lot residential to light industrial. Not much information has come out about the data center itself, and since no proposal has been submitted yet, this entry is considered preliminary activity.

name: Ridgeline Facility
address: Adjacent to the Tucker County Landfill

name: Adams Fork Harless Data Center Energy Campus, Holden
address: Harless Industrial Park, off 22 Mine Road
info: TransGas Development Systems, LLC is proposing a 2.4 GW, 2.8-million-square-foot data center campus in the Harless Industrial Park near Holden as part of the broader Adams Fork Energy Project. The facility is designed as an off-grid data center powered by approximately 2.4 GW of onsite, dispatchable generation, scalable to 9.6 GW. Power would be supplied by a dedicated microgrid natural-gas plant consisting of methane-fired engines with diesel backup fuel stored onsite. The campus is planned to use modular high-reliability infrastructure, dual-fuel capability, and geothermal-assisted cooling supported by a large underlying freshwater reserve. First power is projected within approximately 24 months.

name: Adams Fork Data Center Energy Campus, Wharncliffe
info: TransGas Development Systems, LLC is proposing a second off-grid data center campus near the Twisted Gun Golf Course in Wharncliffe as part of the Adams Fork Energy Project. This site would be powered by a dedicated onsite microgrid natural-gas generation plant composed of approximately 117 methane-fueled engines, with most units operating continuously and diesel backup available from onsite storage tanks if gas supply is interrupted.

name: Monarch Compute Campus and Mountaineer Gigasystem
address: Along W.Va 62, the Ohio River, and CSX Railroad tracks. Around Airport Rd and Hwy 6.
info: Fidelis New Energy is proposing a 2 GW, 2.25-million-square-foot data center campus along the Ohio River, north of Point Pleasant.

name: QTS Kearneysville Data Center Complex
address: Between W.Va Route 115 and CSX Railroad
info: QTS has proposed a new data center campus on a 300-acre site spanning Jefferson and Berkeley Counties, consisting of two Berkeley County parcels for two data center buildings and related infrastructure plus access from Route 115, and a 191-acre industrial-zoned parcel in Jefferson County. Berkeley County has approved the concept plan covering the two buildings, utility structures, and stormwater facilities, while Jefferson County has not yet received development plans. The property was acquired in July 2024, has access to major power and gas infrastructure, will initially use Berkeley County water and sewer service, and remains in the early planning stage according to QTS.

name: Penzance Intelligence Center
info: Penzance Management is proposing a data center on 548 acres of land around the Falling Waters and Bedington areas in Berkeley known as the Bedington Campus. The $4 billion privately funded project would total about 1.9 million square feet at completion, and would deliver at least 600 MW of critical IT capacity as the state’s first designated “High Impact Intelligence Center.” The development is expected to generate roughly 1,000 temporary construction jobs and at least 125 permanent full-time positions, with no direct state funding involved. The campus will likely be grid-connected, with Penzance preparing the site and probably partnering with hyperscale tenants that will ultimately operate within the facility.

name: Google Buffalo Data Center
info: The West Virginia Governor announced on March 27th that Google purchased 1700 acres of land directly north of Buffalo, WV. The plot was previously owned mostly by Franklin Real Estate and was an inactive farm at the time of purchase.

name: Appalachian Mega Site / Hog Lick Data Center
info: Hog Lick Aggregates is planning a large-scale data center campus in Fairmont, West Virginia, on 300 immediately available acres with 1,500+ additional acres for expansion. The site has a path to 1 GW+ power capacity, starting with 150MW in Phase I via limited 138kV upgrades, supported by proposed natural gas pipelines and potential behind-the-meter generation. Phase II scales to 500MW, and Phase III to 1GW through grid and/or on-site power, with 500kV transmission capable of delivering 1GW without upgrades.

name: Quakertown Data Center Park
info: Arrow Real Estate Services is advertising a site for data center development off Rt 309 and Rich Hill Road in Richland Township. According to the advertisement highlights, the site is 155-175 acres, has an available site plan featuring 5+ buildings totaling over 2 million square feet, has "1 GW phased in," and that permits for a gas co-generation plant are in process.

name: Cumru Titus Data Center
companies: Titus Development Co.; Go Energy Group
info: Titus Development Co. and Go Energy Group has expressed interest in developing a 200 MW AI data center campus at the NRG Titus Generating Station, a former coal-fired power plant that was decommissioned in 2014. No development plan has been submitted yet, according to township officials. On the official "Titus Data Center" website, an AI generated mockup displays four data center buildings, an electrical and control building, and a battery energy storage system (BESS).

name: Nottingham Data Center
info: A developer has expressed interest in developing a data center on a 100-plus acre property somewhere on Old Turnpike Road. This would use a closed-loop cooling system. According to the developer, the data center would exist on only approximately 40 acres of the full 100-acre+ site. As of early June, this application/proposal was withdrawn due to community opposition, however the developer stated that they may resubmit a proposal in the future.

name: Andover Data Center
companies: Dykstra Associates, PC; Abbey Beta, LLC
info: Andover Township has passed an ordinance permitting Abbey Beta, LLC, affiliated with the engineering firm Dykstra Associates, to construct a data center in the town's Redevelopment Zone. This zone was established in 2022 and is located off US Route 206. In 2024, an overlay dubbed the Abbey Redevelopment Overlay was passed by the town council encompassing 367 acres, all of which is owned by St. Paul's Abbey. The data center will occupy roughly 9 acres (lot 22.02 in official documents) within this overlay, although Dykstra Associates has been permitted by the Town Council to build on its entirety. The other land is currently planned for new housing, being developed by Abbey Omega, LLC, also affiliated with Dykstra Associates. Recent community backlash has put forth an ordinance to ban data centers within the town as a reaction to the development. Voting is held on May 28th 2026.

name: Jailhouse Studios
address: Former Hamilton County Jail, 601 Walnut Street
info: Urban Story Ventures, a local Chattanoogan company, is redeveloping the former Hamilton County Jail, which is approximately 2 acres, on Walnut Street into "the world's first quantum-ready sovereign data, processing, and AI center and creator campus." \n In mid-March, Hamilton County Commissioners voted to approve the plan. Residents expressed concern at the data center aspect, and a resolution was created and approved stating that data storage shall not exceed 12,000 square feet of the overall project area.

name: Maysville Data Center
info: An undisclosed developer obtained local approval to build a massive 6-building hyperscale data center campus on approximately 2,080 acres of farmland in north of Germantown Road in Mason County. The site sits near East Kentucky Power Cooperative's Hugh L. Spurlock Generating Station and would draw 2.2 gigawatts of power by 2028, with cooling water drawn primarily from the Ohio River. The project promises roughly 400 permanent jobs and a reported capital investment exceeding $555 million, though officials have described it as an over $1 billion investment. This project has drawn significant pushback from residents, many of whom raise concerns about water and electricity demand, as well as the displacement of 28 residents of Meadowland Village, a mobile home park within the project vicinity. These residents received notice from DPD Property Management in March that the park is under contract for sale for the project, and have three months to vacate.

name: Albany Kenwood Data Center
info: The owner of the former Kenwood Convent property is proposing a data center

name: Microsoft La Porte Data Center
info: Microsoft is constructing a $1 billion data center project, consisting of four buildings at 300 Boyd Boulevard. The project was originally announced in 2024 as a 245,000-square-foot data center campus on 489 acres of land, however as of May 2026, Microsoft has a total of ~1300 acres of land for the second campus of 11 buildings, in addition to 6 buildings currently under construction. According to the city, 15% of revenue from the project will go to the LaPorte Community School Corporation over a 20-year period.

name: JPG Data Center
info: In February 2026, the Jefferson County Planning and Zoning Office approved a 9-building data center project across ~2000 acres at the Jefferson Proving Ground, former 55,000-acre U.S. Army munitions testing facility. In May 2026, an appeal challenging the project was denied, allowing the project to move forward.

name: Amazon Wheatfield
address: NIPSCO-owned property, somewhere near the R.M. Schahfer Generating Station
info: Amazon is proposing a $7 billion data center campus on a 304-acre NIPSCO-owned parcel near the R.M. Schahfer Generating Station in Wheatfield. The site was chosen partly for its proximity to existing power infrastructure, which is expected to lower development and operational costs, and the project could generate over $1 billion in local tax revenue over 15 years. \n This project still requires approval from the Jasper County Council and county commissioners, and follows a recent zoning change enabling a $5.7 billion modernization of the nearby generating station, which includes new natural gas and peaker plant facilities to meet rising energy demand.

name: Granville Data Center
companies: Pennsylvania Data Center Partners; PowerHouse Data Centers
info: Pennsylvania Data Center Partners and PowerHouse Data Centers are proposing a data center campus somewhere in Granville, according to a presentation given at a local community meeting on June 1st. According to the developers, the project would support between 130-250 construction jobs, as well as 150 permanent jobs. There is no available information on expected acreage, IT capacity, or water usage.

name: DartPoints Former Lexmark Data Center Redevelopment
info: DartPoints, a Dallas-based data center operator, purchased a former Lexmark data center at 745 W New Circle Road for $29 million. The building is about 343,000 square feet on 29.5 acres. The site includes a substation and with an anticipated IT capacity of 20-30 MW, with expansion potential to 70 MW.

name: Nashville DC Blox Data Center
info: DC BLOX is redeveloping old office buildings at 648 Grassmere Park into a 1-story 69,000-square-foot AI data center. The project would be located adjacent to the Nashville Zoo, and involves demolishing existing buildings on site. This proposal has garnered considerable pushback, particularly from the neighboring zoo, which has created an online petition campaign to pause or halt the development.

name: Muskie Data Campus
info: TeraWulf acquired the Muskie Data Campus in Eastern Kentucky, a hyperscale HPC/AI development site capable of supporting over 1 GW of data center capacity. Phase I is expected to deliver ~500 MW across two buildings, with operations commencing in late 2028 and early 2029. Phase II will bring total campus capacity to ~1,000 MW in 2030, with further expansion potential beyond 1 GW from 2031 onward.

name: Muncy Data Center
address: Between John Brady Drive and Susquehanna River
companies: Danko Holdings II; Fishlips LLC; The Liberty Group
info: Danko Holdings II and Fishlips LLC have submitted an application on April 28th, 2026 for a data center to-be located between John Brady Drive and the Susquehanna River. The project plans feature three data center buildings, six water tanks, two stormwater basins, water and sewage treatment facilities, a substation, and a security building.

name: Gemini Sioux Falls Data Center
address: South of East Rice Street, east of North Veterans Parkway, and west of North Six Mile Road
companies: Gemini Data Center LLC; A1 Development Solutions; Xcel Energy
info: A California-based developer, Gemini Data Center SD LLC, has proposed a hyperscale data center campus on approximately 164 acres northeast of Sioux Falls, adjacent to an existing utility substation and power station. The facility is planned to support up to 500 MW of critical IT load across multiple two-story buildings using a closed-loop cooling system. Power would be supplied by Xcel Energy, which sold the land to the developer. The end tenant has not been disclosed. The site was rezoned light industrial by the Sioux Falls City Council in January 2026, with construction expected to begin in 2026 and operations targeted for 2028. The project is projected to support 50–150 permanent jobs.

name: Sequitor Edge Rapid City
address: Black Hills Industrial Center / 5814 Capacitor Street
companies: Sequitor Edge; Dream Design International; Elevate Rapid City; Black Hills Energy
info: Sequitor Edge, a Nebraska-based edge computing developer, has proposed a modular Tier III colocation facility on approximately 10 acres within the Black Hills Industrial Center on the south side of Rapid City. The initial build will consist of one data hall totaling approximately 30,000 square feet, capable of housing around 240 cabinets at up to 2 MW, with expansion potential to 10 MW across five data halls. The facility will use a closed-loop cooling system requiring a one-time water fill. Groundbreaking is targeted for late summer 2026 with a 14-month construction timeline, and the facility is expected to create 3-5 permanent positions.

name: Polaris Forge 1
info: Applied Digital has developed a 400 MW AI-focused campus on the outskirts of Ellendale. The facility uses a waterless, direct-to-chip liquid cooling system and is fully contracted to CoreWeave under long-term leases totaling multiple buildings. The first 100 MW building reached full operational status in late 2025 across two phases. The campus is designed for a projected PUE of approximately 1.18 and near-zero water usage effectiveness.

name: Polaris Forge 2
companies: Applied Digital; Oracle; Cass County Electric Cooperative; Minnkota Power Cooperative
info: Applied Digital has broken ground on a $3 billion AI campus on more than 900 acres north of Fargo. The initial buildout covers 280 MW across two buildings, with expansion rights extending to a full 1 GW. Power is supplied through Cass County Electric Cooperative, with Applied Digital funding approximately $75 million in grid infrastructure upgrades through Minnkota Power Cooperative. The facility is contracted to an undisclosed U.S.-based hyperscale tenant and is expected to employ more than 200 full-time workers, with initial capacity expected in 2026 and full capacity by early 2027.

name: Applied Digital Oliver County
info: Applied Digital has proposed an AI-focused campus of approximately 430 MW near the town of Center in central North Dakota. The facility would follow the same liquid-cooled design used at the company's existing North Dakota campuses and is expected to require an 18–24 month construction period. The project is projected to create approximately 200 permanent jobs and around 1,000 temporary construction positions. A county-level rezoning moratorium was passed in March 2026 and subsequently repealed in April 2026. A formal rezoning application was filed in May 2026, and the project remains pending county approval.

name: River Run Energy Center
companies: NextEra Energy Resources; Boulevard Associates; Basin Electric Power Cooperative
info: Basin Electric Power Cooperative and NextEra Energy Resources have jointly proposed a combined-cycle natural gas generation facility of approximately 1,450 MW, intended to anchor an associated large-scale data center campus. The project is projected to bring over $18 billion in new investment to the region, generate approximately $15 million in annual property tax revenue for Mercer County, and support around 230 permanent jobs once fully operational. A public open house was held in March 2026 and the partners submitted an application to the Southwest Power Pool in October 2025. Full operations are targeted for 2030.

name: Project Fighting Pike
address: Near the junction of US Highway 2 and the US Highway 85 Bypass
info: Critical Data House LLC, a Los Angeles-based developer, has proposed a 455 MW, seven-building cloud computing campus on 300–400 acres at the northern edge of Williston near the US Highway 2 and Highway 85 interchange. Each building is planned at approximately 65 MW, ranging from 275,000 to 500,000 square feet, and the facility will use closed-loop direct-to-chip liquid cooling. Total projected investment is approximately $10 billion, inclusive of construction and IT hardware. The first building is targeted for operations by end of 2026, with full buildout expected by approximately 2031 across three phases. The City of Williston approved the master plan and planned unit development in March 2025.

name: Atlas Power Williston
companies: FX Solutions Inc.; Atlas Power Group; Mountrail-Williams Electric Cooperative
info: Atlas Power, built by Montana-based FX Solutions, has developed a cryptocurrency mining and high-performance computing facility on approximately 77 acres west of Williston, with Phase 1 consisting of 16 buildings drawing up to 240 MW of power. The full project is planned to scale to 700 MW over multiple phases at a total projected cost of $1.9 billion. Power for the project is supplied by Mountrail-Williams Electric Cooperative. The facility currently draws ~200–240 MW and employs more than 30 permanent workers. The project has faced noise complaints from neighboring properties, and Atlas Power has invested approximately $5 million in acoustic mitigation measures.

name: 4-3 Salem Township Data Center
info: 4-3 Consulting is pursuing a second, larger land deal in Salem Township through a new entity called 4-3 Glennbrook Group. The firm is working with approximately 194 landowners who own a combined 4,000+ acres located to the west of the original QTS parcel, north of Salem Township Boulevard (U.S. Route 11) and west of Stone Church Road. \n The potential payout to landowners is estimated at $1.2 billion, which is more than double the $580 million secured in 4-3's QTS deal. The project would require an estimated 3.5 to 4 gigawatts of electricity, necessitating construction of a new power generation plant and enhanced infrastructure, to be built at the developer's expense. According to the Times Leader, at least 80% of the total 4,000 acres will remain green space, and 39 buildings are anticipated, although this is subject to change. \n A public hearing regarding the application is expected sometime in July or August.

name: Holyoke Data Center
info: Boston-based Chestnut River Power and Infrastructure is proposing a $200 million, 20-MW data center at the vacant Hampden Paper complex. This smaller data center project intends to utilize Holyoke's low-cost hydroelectric power.

name: Project Domino
address: West of Interstate 65, around State Road 32, in the southern portion of the ~9,000-acre LEAP District
info: Meta is currently constructing 'Project Domino', a hyperscale, 1-GW data center campus at the LEAP (Limitless Exploration/Advanced Pace) Research and Innovation District in Lebanon, representing an investment of over $10 billion, making it one of Meta's largest infrastructure investments to date (according to Meta in February 2026). The first phase is anticipated to come online by late 2027 or early 2028, with construction continuing through multiple subsequent phases. The campus includes 15 buildings—12 data centers, one network building, one logistics warehouse building, and one administrative building.

name: Gravel Pike Data Center
address: Off Gravel Pike Road, Southeast of Ziegler Road
info: Hanover development, L.P. filed an application for a 150,000-square-foot data center on a 37.476-acre tract of light industrial-zoned farmland off Gravel Pike, near Ziegler Road. \n This application arrived hours before the township passed an ordinance defining data center as a conditional use.

name: Sharon Keel Infrastructure Data Center Expansion
info: Keel Infrastructure, formerly known as Bitfarms, is advancing a data center expansion on Clark Street on a roughly 17-acre former industrial brownfield site associated with the old Westinghouse Electric plant. The plan is to convert the existing ~30-MW Bitcoin-mining operation into a 110 MW AI/high-performance-computing campus, spanning parcels on both the north and south sides of the street with multiple buildings. The developer has stated it would begin construction and hiring before the end of 2026, contingent on remaining approvals from the Mercer County Regional Planning Commission, Sharon Planning Commission, and city council, with the facility potentially operational by late 2027 or early 2028.

name: PNK Valley View Data Center
info: PNK Group, a NYC-based developer with Russian affiliation, has expressed interest in developing a 44.5-acre site along Archbald Heights Road into a data center. In mid-July, the company published a public survey in an effort to gauge local interest in this development. Currently, there is little information about capacity figures or whether or not an application has been submitted to Archbald Borough.

name: Harper Road Technology Park
info: Proposed $1.5B multi-building campus in Peculiar (Cass County). Surfaced via opposition tracker: city added data-center zoning definition enabling the project; organized resident opposition followed (2024-10 zoning fight). Coordinates approximate (city centroid). Sources: opposition tracker records; harborcountry/local coverage pending.

name: Province Group Perry Village Data Center Campus
info: Seven-building campus on 163 acres; developer had agreed to purchase land from Perry Village for $8.4M. Zoning application formally withdrawn July 2025 per opposition tracker records. Announced date not verified; coordinates approximate (village centroid).

name: Meta Hyperion
info: Meta's multi-billion-dollar Richland Parish AI campus announced December 2024; under construction. Reported multi-GW scale; capacity left blank pending sourced figure. Associated federal scrutiny (Senate EPW inquiry over Entergy gas plants) tracked in opposition records. Coordinates approximate.

name: Prado AI Industrial Campus
companies: Prado AI; PraCon Global Investment Group
info: Proposed AI data center + semiconductor fabrication campus with dedicated 350 MW gas plant (plant capacity, not IT load). PSC declaratory-opinion request filed 2026-03-02; PSC declined to opine 2026-05-29 calling request premature; developer says project proceeding, site now described as Jackson metro, undisclosed. Sources: Mississippi Today 2026-04-22 and 2026-06-01; WLBT 2026-05-29.

name: New Carlisle Chicago Trail Data Center (Third Site)
address: Chicago Trail between Spruce Rd and Walnut Rd
info: ~$12-13B proposal on 1,057 acres of farmland; developer undisclosed (petition entity tied to 80 Broad St NY address; land owned by Mall-O-Manor LLC / Szczypiorski family). Rezoning DENIED 7-2 by St. Joseph County Council 2025-12-09 after unfavorable Area Plan Commission and Land Use Planning Committee recommendations; renewed petition activity first-read 2026-05-13 per tracker. Phase reflects active petition; the 2025-12-09 denial is a sourced adverse milestone. Sources: South Bend Tribune / WSBT / WNDU Dec 2025; DCD May 2026.

name: Project Maize / Google Michigan City Data Center
info: $832M conversion of former Federal Mogul facility (400,000 sq ft brownfield). Plans submitted to Common Council early 2025 (date precision: year); council approved four resolutions Sept 2025; construction began fall 2025; Google confirmed acquisition 2026-04-16. Opposition: IUOE Local 150 labor protests and picketing. Sources: WNDU/WSBT 2025-2026, Building Indiana Sept 2025.

name: Meta Project Everest
info: Second Meta data center campus in Richland Parish, separate from Hyperion. Surfaced via LPSC filing: Meta subsidiary Evest LLC applied 2026-03-25 for approval of generation/transmission resources tied to a 20-year contract (Docket per tracker record). Exact site undisclosed; coordinates are parish-approximate. Source: opposition tracker LPSC record.

name: Terra Nexus Custer Avenue Data Center
info: 200 MW data center; plans submitted to Fayetteville fall 2025 (date precision: month approximated to Oct). Sited near the Fayetteville PWC power plant. Coordinates approximate. Source: CityView NC 2026-05-22.

name: PRSM Group Cumberland County Data Center
info: PRSM Group (Charlotte/Arlington VA) client project: two 1M-sq-ft phases, 225 MW, on 50 acres in an undisclosed Cumberland County industrial park. Publicly disclosed by partner Eric Little at the county's 2026-05-18 moratorium hearing, where he argued against the moratorium; county adopted a 6-month moratorium for unincorporated areas 2026-06-15. Source: CityView NC 2026-05-22 and 2026-06-16.

name: Project Sail Data Center
address: Wagers Mill Road / Welcome Sargent Road
info: Hyperscale campus, 9 buildings ~4.34M sq ft, ~900 MW, on ~829 acres near Newnan. Atlas Development first filed rezoning December 2024 (rural conservation to industrial); amended January 2026 after county adopted its data center ordinance Dec 2025. Coweta County Board of Commissioners approved the rezoning 3-2 on 2026-04-07. Prologis agreed to buy the site May 2025. 17 residents filed a Superior Court appeal 2026-05-05 seeking to void the rezoning — finality watch. Coordinates approximate. Sources: Atlanta News First 2026-04-08, The Citizen 2026-04-10, DeSmog 2026-04-07, CBS Atlanta 2026-05-07.

name: Howell Township Data Center (Project Splitrock)
info: ~1,077-acre conditional rezoning (agricultural to Research & Technology) for a $1B single-story data-processing campus by Randee LLC / Stantec, early-stage codename 'Project Splitrock'; a township trustee alleged Meta as the end client (unconfirmed). Announced/filed for the 2025-09-23 Planning Commission hearing. Howell Township Planning Commission voted unanimously against rezoning 2025-09-23; Livingston County Planning Commission unanimously against 2025-11-19; township enacted a 6-month moratorium 2025-11-20 (worded to exempt this request). Developer WITHDREW the rezoning request and zoning text amendment ahead of the 2025-12-08 township board meeting. Outcome blocked_confirmed via withdrawal (not a board denial vote). Coordinates approximate. Sources: Planet Detroit 2025-12-08, FracTracker 2026-01, DCD 2026-03-31, Michigan Advance 2025-11-21.

name: Natelli Vance County Data Center
address: US-158 Business, Triangle North Vance, near Henderson
info: Rezoning of ~113 acres (three parcels) from Employment & Institutional to Light Industrial along US-158 Business, part of a larger ~619-acre corridor; no end user named, widely expected to be a data center (same developer as the withdrawn Apex/New Hill project). Faced organized opposition (moratorium petition, ~150 residents at the April 6 hearing). Vance County Planning Board approved unanimously in March 2026; Board of Commissioners delayed at the April 6 meeting, then approved 6-1 at a special-called meeting on 2026-04-20. Coordinates approximate. Sources: WRAL 2026-04-20, Henderson Dispatch 2026-06-06, DCD 2026-05-07.
